tweets.text:
The devil is in the details. 
This chart is making the rounds supporting the claims that younger generations are struggling relative to previous generations.  But read closer the analysis:
- Taking as a baseline the Greatest Generation, which was born between 1900 and 1927, 
-the Silent Generation earned 34% more, 
- the Boomers made a cumulative 70% more, 
- Gen X took home 97% more and 
- Millennials brought in 133% more than the Greatest Generation even when adjusted for inflation. 

The data also shows that 
- the Silent Generation worked 14% more hours than the generation before 
- and Boomers worked another 14% more. 
- However, working hours have been relatively stable for generations since.
@adamtaggart

tweets.text:
Yesterday, we noted that ETF flows have exceeded $1 Trillion in the first half of this year (~40% above the previous annual record).  US Listed ETF launches are also running well above trend with 770 launches this year and 5400 in existence. (There are more ETFs than publicly traded equities).
I am sure there is a warning in here somewhere. 🤔

tweets.text:
Strait of Hormuz over the last 24 hours.

US successfully escorted one group of tankers in through the Oman lane.

All of them had their AIS off, but there was one Indian cargo ship that had it on when it went through. The list can be seen below.

Following two days of US strikes, the US is back to escorting ships in. My guess is that it will try to escort a group out tonight, and Iran can either respond and create more escalation going into the weekend or it can lose leverage.

The Oman lane will be heavily contested in the coming days, which leaves either side with no choice but to escalate.

I have included the video from @MarineTraffic.
